etymology.description: śąą(śąą) (gńď, "spear") + śąą(śąą) (gńď, "spear") facing each other -> original form of śģė(śģė) (c√°n, "to damage; to destroy") -> one spear rotated
etymology.interpretation: The character śąĒ is an ideogrammic compound depicting two spears, symbolizing mutual confrontation or destruction, which relates to its original meaning of 'damaging' or 'destroying'.
etymology.simplification: The traditional character śąĒ was simplified to śąč by reducing the number of strokes in the representation of spears.
